video_title: Trainwreck: Balloon Boy | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
I find it funny when they say it's 'global news' when no one across the pond even knows anything about this story. So neither did I. It was entertaining for sure! I can't even believe though that anyone thought the boy was actually in there. He would have fallen out within minutes after departure. I just knew from the start he'd show up at a certain moment. And then about the alleged lying, at first with the boy's slip up I thought he maybe could have misunderstood, but after the polygraph test(s) it seemed kinda obvious. They were probably trying to get attention in order to get a family reality show. I just don't get how the father thought him screaming like a nutter and the footage of his previous messed up acting-like-a-child-with-a-tantrum behaviour on that Wife Swap reality show would make them great candidates for their own reality show!
Then again, I heard about other cases where US police departments were trying to save face by doing everything they can to get someone convicted who was in fact innocent. So I guess you never know. Threatening to deport the wife to Japan if the husband didn't sign the plea deal is rather vile and reeks of extortion imo.

comment_text: The family was interviewed on national TV a few days after the balloon landed.  The child was asked a question about why daddy was building a balloon. The 6 year old honestly answered " Because we were going to be on TV". Before that statement, the dad was saying he was just building a large balloon to show his kids what they were capable of. Dad also said that the balloon was never intended to have humans in it.  This led to the theory that it was all staged and the dad knew the child was not in the balloon and was "hiding" in the house the whole time.
